steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to "emphasize orthoclase's Mohs scale number." That means the correct choice must (1) state orthoclase's number explicitly and (2) give the number meaning — showing where it falls on the scale relative to other minerals.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "In the Mohs scale of mineral hardness, corundum (9) is ranked higher than fluorite (4).": focuses on corundum and fluorite; orthoclase is not mentioned at all. ✗
  • "Orthoclase has a Mohs scale number of 6, which means that it is harder than fluorite (4) but softer than corundum (9).": states orthoclase's number (6) and immediately contextualizes it by comparing to both a lower (fluorite 4) and a higher (corundum 9) mineral, directly emphasizing the number's meaning. ✓
  • "Orthoclase, fluorite, and corundum can be ordered by their ability to leave visible scratches on other minerals.": mentions orthoclase but never states its Mohs number. ✗
  • "Corundum, which has a Mohs scale number of 9, can scratch both fluorite and orthoclase.": emphasizes corundum's number, not orthoclase's. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
